t6.subject: SARIF output for vuln scan
t6.description: Add a SARIF 2.1.0 emitter that converts VulnReport into SARIF JSON, and wire a --sarif <path> (or --format sarif) flag into `codegraph analyze vuln`. This unlocks the GitHub Advanced Security integration story from the MDASH demo. Add a functional test asserting valid SARIF shape (runs[].tool.driver.rules + results[] with ruleId, level, message, locations).
t6.acceptance_criteria: cargo test passes a sarif serialization test; `codegraph analyze vuln --sarif out.sarif` writes a file with runs[0].results populated and tool.driver.rules listing the template kinds.

t3.subject: Analyze ~/.config/jfc/sessions for anomalies (161 consecutive identical calls)
t3.description: Dig into session JSONs: characterize the consecutive-identical tool calls (retries? truncated outputs causing re-calls?), failed Edit/TaskDone rates, pending tool calls.

t5.subject: Wire MustFollow template (kill dead enum)
t5.description: TemplateKind::MustFollow and DeviantFrequency are defined but never emitted. Map the concurrency must-follow-shaped findings (LossySend, SplitStateTransition, LossyThenCommitted = operation-A-without-follow-B) onto TemplateKind::MustFollow so the taxonomy matches MDASH's "operation A occurs but required follow B is absent" and the template is no longer dead. Keep the human-facing rule id but tag the template/class.
t5.acceptance_criteria: vuln findings from concurrency carry MustFollow template semantics; existing vuln tests still pass; count/pin tests updated if needed.

t4.subject: MCP classify/verify loop (model proposes, graph proves)
t4.description: Realize the unbuilt LLM half of the vuln engine through the MCP/agent layer (which has model access) rather than adding live HTTP deps to the core crate — matching the design doc in vuln/classify.rs. Add an MCP tool that (a) surfaces candidate functions for role classification, and/or (b) accepts agent-supplied RoleProposals, runs them through the existing GraphVerifier, and emits verified findings tagged InferenceOrigin::Llm. Turns the dead GraphVerifier (0 callers) and InferenceOrigin::Llm into a production path.
t4.acceptance_criteria: A new MCP tool is registered (count/name guard updated), a handler bridges to GraphVerifier, and a tests/mcp_tools_test.rs case exercises propose->verify->finding. cargo test --workspace green.

t7.subject: HTML report for vuln scan
t7.description: Add an HTML report emitter rendering VulnReport (summary counts by domain/severity + per-finding table with file:line, class, confidence, message) and wire a --html <path> flag into `codegraph analyze vuln`. Mirrors the MDASH "HTML report I can give to management" demo beat.
t7.acceptance_criteria: `codegraph analyze vuln --html out.html` writes a self-contained HTML file containing the finding count and a table row per finding; a unit test asserts the HTML contains expected summary + escaping.

t1.subject: General bug hunt in codegraph MCP server hot paths
t1.description: Audit explore/search/node handlers for correctness bugs: unwraps, panics, lossy UTF-8 slicing, bad caps, error swallowing.
t1.activeForm: Hunting bugs in MCP hot paths

t2.subject: Audit MCP output truncation/capping logic in codegraph-rs
t2.description: Find where MCP tool responses are built/capped/truncated in codegraph-rs and look for bugs (off-by-one, byte vs char truncation, silent drops, missing staleness banners).
